name: IPG PHOTONICS CORP
business: IPG Photonics Corporation develops, manufactures, and sells high-performance fiber lasers, fiber amplifiers, diode lasers, and laser-based systems. These products are used for diverse applications in materials processing, medical, and advanced applications. The company sells globally to original equipment manufacturers, system integrators, and end users.

exposures.sentence: Our high power pulsed lasers are used in thin foil cutting applications in electric vehicle battery production.

We manufacture complete medical laser systems and consumable fibers primarily for urology applications, including benign prostatic hyperplasia and lithotripsy, based on our thulium fiber laser technology.

Separately, we have developed and launched CROSSBOW , a counter-unmanned aircraft system (counter UAS) solution offered to government and other authorized parties as part of our advanced application offerings.
Representative applications include directed energy, spectroscopy, optical trapping, remote sensing, LIDAR, semiconductor metrology and inspection, and materials characterization.
Biomedical analytical instruments, metrology, disinfection/sterilization, environmental and security monitoring, quantum computing
Our principal end markets and representative applications for lasers within those markets include: ... Aerospace, Rail and Shipbuilding
Energy storage battery: welding, cleaning, slurry drying, ablation and texturing, cutting, including foil cutting

Fiber lasers are utilized in materials processing applications requiring very high power densities, such as cutting, welding, marking, engraving, additive manufacturing, ablation and cleaning, printing, drilling and cladding.
Our information systems are subject to cyber-attacks, interruptions and failures.

We invested $78.8 million, $98.5 million and $110.5 million in capital expenditures in 2025, 2024 and 2023, respectively. Most of this investment relates to the expansion of our manufacturing capacity.

catalysts.sentence: Hearings were held in January 2026 and we await the decisions of the two UPC panels on February 24 and March 16, 2026.

catalysts.event: U.S. Supreme Court ruling on tariffs
catalysts.sentence: On February 20, 2026, the U.S. Supreme Court rendered a decision invalidating tariffs imposed under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act.

catalysts.event: OECD Pillar Two side-by-side package release
catalysts.sentence: On January 5, 2026, the OECD released a comprehensive package introducing a side-by-side arrangement in relation to Pillar Two.

catalysts.event: Enactment of One Big Beautiful Bill Act
catalysts.sentence: On July 4, 2025, the U.S. enacted H.R. 1 "A bill to provide for reconciliation pursuant to Title II of H. Con. Res. 14", commonly referred to as the One Big Beautiful Bill Act ("OBBBA").
